column1: Original
column2: English Translation
column3: Meaning

column1: A di laass ounce a strent teck yu ova di hill
column2: It is the last ounce of strength that takes you over the hill.
column3: Success often requires pushing through when you are most exhausted. Perseverance in the final, hardest stretch is what leads to victory.

column1: A nuh di smaddy weh nuh like yu a prablem. A di smaddy weh gwaan like dem like yu
column2: It’s not the person who doesn’t like you that’s the problem. It's the person who pretends to like you
column3: An open enemy is harmless compared to a fake friend who secretly wishes you ill while pretending to support you.

column1: Big man nuh dreem seh him a guh a tilet. A bway ting dat
column2: Big men don’t dream of going to the bathroom. That's a boy's thing
column3: Mature adults should take responsibility and maintain control over their lives, leaving childish mistakes or excuses behind.

column1: Check fi yu life an yu naah guh spread mischiff pon nubady. Yu ave fi yu prablem tu
column2: Examine your life and you will not go spread mischief on anybody. You have your problems too.
column3: Focus on your own flaws and personal challenges instead of gossiping or causing trouble for others. People who live in glass houses shouldn't throw stones.

column1: Dem a look fi yu mistake an miss yu message
column2: They are looking for your mistake and miss your message
column3: People are often so eager to criticize how you deliver something that they completely miss the valuable point you are making.

column1: Evrytime yu git up yu fi tank Gad fi him day
column2: Every time you get up you should thank God for his day.
column3: Practice daily gratitude for the gift of life. Waking up each morning is a blessing that should never be taken for granted.

column1: Gad a teck time fi gii yu. Nuh rush it
column2: God is taking time to give to you. Don’t rush it
column3: Have patience and trust the process. Your blessings will come at the right time, so do not force things.

column1: Guh easy an yu head wii always keep clear an yu haat naah full
column2: Relax and take it easy and your head will always keep clear and your heart will not be full
column3: Taking a calm, measured approach to life prevents mental burnout and emotional overwhelm.

column1: Haad wuk bill wackle an Daab. Dreem meck yu sleep a door
column2: Hard work builds wattle and daub. Dreams make you sleep outside
column3: Action and effort yield tangible results and security, whereas merely dreaming without working leaves you with nothing.

column1: If yu reely waan live spen nuff time wid chrue family
column2: If you really want to live spend a lot of time with true family
column3: Spending quality time with loved ones is essential for a fulfilling and happy life.

column1: If yu tink tumuch yu brain wi badda yu
column2: If you think too much your brain will bother you
column3: Overthinking leads to unnecessary stress and anxiety. Sometimes it is better to let things be.

column1: Life beet yu up wen yu stap chry. Truss yusself an Gad. Yu aguh deh pon top all di while
column2: Life betrays you when you stop trying. Trust yourself and God. You will be on top all the time
column3: Giving up leads to defeat. Perseverance, self-belief, and faith are the keys to long-term success.

column1: Likkle gud vibes meck Big difference
column2: Good vibes can make a big difference
column3: Small acts of positivity, kindness, and good energy can have a massive impact on a situation or someone's day.

column1: Lion nuh look back wen mongrel dawg baak
column2: The Lion does not pay attention to the mongrel dog barking
column3: Confident, successful people do not let the petty insults or noise of insignificant critics distract them.

column1: Man weh share dem prablem wid yu wi guh a deep wid yu
column2: Men who share their problems with you will go in depth with you
column3: Vulnerability builds strong bonds; someone willing to share their struggles is offering true, profound friendship.

column1: Meck wi tap bigup wisself an gwaan wid iigle chat
column2: Let's stop boasting and persisting with idle chatter
column3: It is better to be humble and productive than to waste time bragging and engaging in empty talk.

column1: Mistake weh umble yu betta dan big wuk weh meck yu haaty
column2: A mistake that humbles you is better than a big job that makes you haughty
column3: It is better to fail and learn humility than to succeed and become painfully arrogant. Character matters more than status.

column1: Nubady nuh affi push yu wen yu fine yu callin
column2: Nobody has to push you when you find your calling.
column3: True passion provides its own motivation. When you discover your life's purpose, you will be naturally driven to pursue it without needing external pressure.

column1: Nuhbady cyaan replace yu. Nuhbady cyaan meck two a yu. A yu one meck
column2: Nobody can replace you. Nobody can make two of you. Only one of you was made.
column3: You are completely unique and irreplaceable. It is a reminder to embrace your individuality and recognize your inherent self-worth.

column1: Nuh cya how sum peeple si di fire light. It affi bun dem fi dem move
column2: Don't care how some people see the fire light. It has to burn them for them to move.
column3: Some people cannot learn from warnings or by observing danger; they have to experience the painful consequences themselves before they change their behavior. Experience is a harsh, but necessary, teacher for some.

column1: Nuh kip yu door open fi peeple weh lack fi dem pon yu
column2: Do not keep your door open for people who lock theirs on you.
column3: Protect your peace and set boundaries. Do not remain endlessly available or generous to people who do not reciprocate your kindness or who shut you out when you are in need.

column1: Nuh wurry wen badmine judge yu cause mongrel dawg baak offa people dem nuh know
column2: Do not worry when bad-minded people judge you because a mongrel dog barks at people they do not know.
column3: Do not let the criticism of jealous or envious people bother you. People often attack what they do not understand or cannot relate to, much like a stray dog barking at a stranger.

column1: One ting fi larn fram life; yu Hart aguh bruck. It aguh pain yu. But yu aguh gyet love
column2: One thing to learn from life; your Heart will be broken. It will hurt. But you will find love
column3: Pain and heartbreak are inevitable parts of the human experience, but they are worth enduring to experience true love and joy.

column1: Peeple weh nuh tankful seh dem nevva ask yu fi du nutten fi dem
column2: Ungrateful people say they never asked you to do anything for them
column3: Ungrateful people will try to invalidate your kindness and avoid showing appreciation by claiming they didn't need your help anyway.

column1: Pickney seh wha him hear a yaad
column2: A Child says what he hears at home
column3: Children are a product of their environment and mimic what they see and hear from their parents.

column1: Same hurricane weh mash yu up clear road fi yu
column2: The same hurricane that mashes you up clears the road for you.
column3: Disruptive events or major setbacks often remove long-standing obstacles from your life, creating a fresh, clear path forward. Destruction often paves the way for new opportunities.

column1: Stay inna yu lane. Busha cane truck wi run yu ova
column2: Stay in your lane. The overseer's (Busha's) cane truck will run you over.
column3: Mind your own business and stick to what concerns you. Interfering in matters above your level or outside your domain will only lead to your downfall.

column1: Sumtime it good fi listen weh people nuh seh
column2: Sometimes it is good to listen to what people do not say.
column3: Pay close attention to the unsaid. Silence, omissions, and body language often reveal much more about a person's true intentions than their spoken words.

column1: Wen Gad fling sumpmn pon yu ketch it
column2: When God throws something at you you catch it
column3: Always be ready and grateful to accept the blessings and opportunities that life presents to you.

column1: Wen yu fine guud peeple yu fine sinting weh haad fi fine
column2: When you find good people you find something that is hard to find
column3: Genuine, kind-hearted people are rare, so you should value and cherish them when they come into your life.

column1: Wet wedda fren a fren. A dry wedda fren a nuh fren
column2: A wet weather friend is a friend. A dry weather friend is not a friend.
column3: True friends stand by you during hard times (the storms or "wet weather"), while those who are only around when things are easy and prosperous ("dry weather") are not real friends.

column1: When yu spen money pon yusself fi meck a money dung di road. A di bess ting yu evva du. Nuhbadda tun roun tell nuhbady seh yu sarry
column2: When you spend money on yourself to make money down the road. It is the best thing you ever do. Do not bother turning around to tell anybody that you are sorry.
column3: Investing in yourself and your skills for future returns is a highly strategic move. Never apologize to others for prioritizing your own long-term growth and success.

column1: Yu fi cum eeen like Rosemary bush. People wi glad fi deh roun yu
column2: You should be just like the Rosemary bush. People will be happy to be around you
column3: Cultivate a pleasant and uplifting personality so that others naturally enjoy your company and presence.

column1: Yu must always a check pon yu fren dem cause dem might a drink widout yu
column2: You must always check on your friends because they might be drinking without you.
column3: A lighthearted reminder to actively maintain your social connections and check in on your circle, ensuring you stay involved and do not drift apart.
